observatory_hook: Source-family saturation map; comparator baseline panel; taxonomy/methods legend
interpretation_note: Comparator and methods families are well represented and useful for framing, but they should not be read as final authority on event truth or legal status.

observatory_hook: Security/agentic crosswalk; remediation-visibility panel; issuer-vs-independent-source facet
interpretation_note: These families strengthen mechanism and remediation analysis, but they still sit below higher-authority event anchors.

observatory_hook: Authority-weighted evidence matrix; regulator-action timeline; jurisdiction map
interpretation_note: This is the broadest official-action family already supporting bounded shared claims, but it remains geographically concentrated.

observatory_hook: Route-B blocker board; hostile-reviewer bottleneck stack; source-family saturation map overlay
interpretation_note: These are the highest-value thin families: sf08 remains the court-depth blocker, sf09 the disclosure-to-proof blocker, and sf11 the peer-reviewed-security blocker.

observatory_hook: Jurisdiction/language map; lead-to-authority funnel; rare-language methods note
interpretation_note: This family is publication-useful because it exposes non-English discovery and translation friction, but it still needs stronger direct-authority upgrades for stronger normative claims.

observatory_hook: Research-integrity source-depth panel; document-depth ladder; procedural-contamination sidecar
interpretation_note: This family is already reviewer-legible because it separates direct notice, mediated notice, and weak/gated rows instead of flattening them.

observatory_hook: Lead queue; novelty/authority matrix; residual-category stress-test panel
interpretation_note: These families define LIMEN's novelty frontier and now sit in two different governed states: sf14 remains an implemented empty lead queue below seeded use, while sf15 has one governed residual queue row still held below seeded use and shared claims.

observatory_hook: Route-B experiment selector; blocker-order badge; reviewer-facing limitations note
interpretation_note: The blocker order is unchanged in this cycle. The new delta is visual/provenance currentness repair so Figure 1, dashboard cells, and provenance-facing surfaces reflect the live frontier-governance state rather than older empty-queue wording.
